name: date-utils
description: Date utility library built on dayjs providing DateTime creation, parsing (including relative expressions like 'now-1d'), timezone utilities, duration, and locale settings.
when_to_use: Date utility library built on dayjs providing DateTime creation, parsing (including relative expressions like 'now-1d'), timezone utilities, duration, and locale settings
avoid: Not for rendering date pickers — use @gravity-ui/date-components. Not a React component library — pure utility functions.
not_for: Not for rendering date pickers — use @gravity-ui/date-components. Not a React component library — pure utility functions.

name: dateTime
description: Creates a DateTime object from various input formats (string, number, array, object). Supports time zone, locale-aware formatting, and arithmetic. Built on dayjs.
when_to_use: Creating a DateTime instance from a known input value
Formatting dates with time zone awareness
Performing date arithmetic (add, subtract, diff)
avoid: Parsing relative expressions like 'now-1d' — use dateTimeParse instead
signature: dateTime(options?: { input?: DateTimeInput; format?: string; timeZone?: string; lang?: string }): DateTime

parameters.name: options.input
parameters.description: Input value: string, number (unix ms), array, or object.

parameters.name: options.format
parameters.description: Format string for parsing the input (e.g. 'YYYY-MM-DD').

name: dateTimeParse
description: Parses a date from various formats including absolute values, arrays, objects, and relative expressions like 'now-1d'. Returns undefined for invalid input.
when_to_use: Parsing user-provided date strings that may be absolute or relative
Handling 'now', 'now-1d', 'now/d' style date math expressions
Safe parsing that returns undefined on invalid input
avoid: When input is always a known valid format — use dateTime instead for clarity
signature: dateTimeParse(input: DateTimeInput | string, options?: { timeZone?: string; format?: string; lang?: string }): DateTime | undefined

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Date input: string (absolute or relative like 'now-1d'), number, array, or object.

parameters.name: options.timeZone
parameters.description: IANA time zone for interpreting the input.

name: dateTimeUtc
description: Creates a DateTime object in UTC mode. Equivalent to dateTime but forces UTC time zone interpretation.
when_to_use: Working with dates that must be interpreted in UTC regardless of user locale
avoid: When time zone awareness is needed — use dateTime with timeZone option instead
signature: dateTimeUtc(options?: { input?: DateTimeInput; format?: string }): DateTime

name: duration
description: Creates a Duration object representing a span of time. Supports ISO 8601 duration strings and unit-based construction. Provides getters, math, and formatting methods.
when_to_use: Representing and formatting time durations (e.g. 2 hours 30 minutes)
Parsing ISO 8601 duration strings like 'P1DT2H'
avoid: Date arithmetic between two specific dates — use DateTime.diff instead

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: ISO 8601 string, number (ms), or object with unit keys.

name: expandFormat
description: Expands a localized or shorthand date format token (such as 'L', 'LL', 'LT') to its full format string for the current or specified locale. Useful for building locale-aware format strings dynamically.
when_to_use: Resolving shorthand format tokens ('L', 'LL', 'LT', 'LTS') to full format strings for a given locale
Building dynamic format strings based on user locale
avoid: When you already know the full format string — use it directly in dateTime or dateTimeParse

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format token or string to expand (e.g. 'L', 'LL', 'LT').

parameters.name: locale
parameters.description: Locale string to use for expansion. Defaults to the current settings locale.

name: getTimeZonesList
description: Returns an array of all supported IANA time zone identifiers for use in timezone selection UIs.
when_to_use: Populating a timezone picker dropdown

name: guessUserTimeZone
description: Detects the user's local IANA time zone string using the Intl API (e.g. 'Europe/Berlin').
when_to_use: Initializing a time zone picker with the user's local zone
Defaulting date displays to the user's system timezone
avoid: Server-side rendering where no browser Intl is available — provide a fallback

name: HTML5_INPUT_FORMATS
description: Constant array of date format strings compatible with HTML5 input elements (date, datetime-local, time, week, month). Useful for parsing values from HTML form inputs.
when_to_use: Passing as the format option when parsing values from native HTML5 date/time inputs

name: isDuration
description: Type guard that checks whether a value is a Duration instance created by the date-utils library.
when_to_use: Checking whether an unknown value is a Duration before calling duration methods on it

name: isDateTime
description: Type guard that checks whether a value is a DateTime instance created by the date-utils library.
when_to_use: Checking whether an unknown value is a DateTime before calling DateTime methods on it

name: isLikeRelative
description: Checks whether a string looks like a relative date expression (e.g. 'now', 'now-1d', 'now/w'). Does not fully validate the expression — use dateTimeParse for validation.
when_to_use: Quickly determining if a user-provided date string is relative before parsing
Branching logic between absolute and relative date handling
avoid: Full validation — use dateTimeParse and check for undefined instead

name: isValid
description: Checks whether a date input can be successfully parsed into a valid DateTime. Returns false for invalid strings, null, undefined, or unparseable values.
when_to_use: Validating user-supplied date strings before using them
Form validation for date fields
avoid: Validating time zones — use isValidTimeZone instead
signature: isValid(input: DateTimeInput | string, options?: { format?: string; timeZone?: string }): boolean

parameters.name: options.timeZone
parameters.description: IANA time zone to use during parsing.

name: isValidTimeZone
description: Checks whether a given string is a valid IANA time zone identifier.
when_to_use: Validating user-supplied or URL-derived time zone strings before use

parameters.name: timeZone
parameters.description: IANA time zone string to validate.

name: settings
description: Global settings singleton for date-utils. Controls active locale, locale loading, and locale customization (e.g. first day of week). Must be configured before rendering date-aware components.
when_to_use: Setting the active locale for date formatting
Loading a new locale dynamically (e.g. on language switch)
Customizing locale properties like first day of week
avoid: Calling setLocale before loadLocale resolves — locale must be loaded first

name: timeZoneOffset
description: Returns the UTC offset in minutes for a given IANA time zone at a specific point in time. Positive values are east of UTC.
when_to_use: Computing the UTC offset for a time zone at a specific date/time
Displaying timezone offsets in a timezone picker

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Date at which to compute the offset (to handle DST). Defaults to current time.

name: UtcTimeZone
description: Constant string representing the UTC time zone identifier ('UTC'). Use this instead of a raw string to avoid typos when passing UTC as a time zone.
when_to_use: Passing UTC as a time zone to dateTime, dateTimeParse, or other date-utils functions
